step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the assessment. Radiation-induced thyroid cancer fits squarely into the definition of a somatic effect because it directly damages somatic tissues—specifically thyrocytes—and triggers genetic changes leading to tumor formation within an individual’s lifetime rather than being inherited genetically or affecting developing embryos/fetuses. There is no need to introduce any further reasons since the existing explanation comprehensively covers all aspects relevant to classifying this condition properly according to its mechanism of action post-radiation exposure. Therefore, my final answer remains:

Answer Choice: A.
